name: github-copilot-llm-gateway
description: Connect GitHub Copilot to open-source models via vLLM or any OpenAI-compatible server

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: Optional bearer token sent to the inference server. Leave blank for unauthenticated local servers (vLLM, Ollama, llama.cpp). Stored by VS Code; takes precedence over the 'Configure Server' command's SecretStorage entry.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.serverUrl.description: Inference server URL (OpenAI API compatible endpoint)

github.copilot.llm-gateway.apiKey.description: Deprecated. Use the 'Configure Server' command to store the API key in VS Code's secret storage. Any value entered here is migrated and cleared on extension activation.
github.copilot.llm-gateway.apiKey.deprecationMessage: API keys are now stored in VS Code's secret storage. Use the 'GitHub Copilot LLM Gateway: Configure Server' command to set the API key.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.defaultMaxTok.description: Fallback context window size (input tokens) when the server does not report it

github.copilot.llm-gateway.defaultMaxOut.description: Fallback maximum output tokens when the server does not report context size

github.copilot.llm-gateway.enableImageIn.description: Enable image input capability for models that support multimodal requests

github.copilot.llm-gateway.enableToolCal.description: Enable tool calling capability for models

github.copilot.llm-gateway.parallelToolC.description: Allow model to call multiple tools in parallel (disable if model has issues with parallel calls)

github.copilot.llm-gateway.agentTemperat.description: Temperature for agent/tool calling mode (lower = more consistent tool call formatting). Set to 0.0 for best results with fine-tuned models.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.verboseLoggin.description: When enabled, the full chat request body (including messages and tool args) is written to the output channel. Keep disabled unless you are reproducing an issue — captured logs may contain conversation content from the user's current editor.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.customHeaders.description: Deprecated. Use the 'Edit Custom Headers' command to store headers in VS Code's secret storage. Any value entered here is migrated and cleared on extension activation.
github.copilot.llm-gateway.customHeaders.deprecationMessage: Custom headers (which may carry credentials such as 'Authorization') are now stored in VS Code's secret storage. Use the 'GitHub Copilot LLM Gateway: Edit Custom Headers' command.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.extraModelOpt.description: Extra parameters merged into the chat-completions request body (e.g. top_k, repetition_penalty, seed). Per-request modelOptions from the caller take precedence. Edit in settings.json for non-string values.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.perModelOptio.description: Per-model chat-completion parameters, keyed by model id. Merged on top of 'extraModelOptions' for matching models; per-request modelOptions from the caller still take precedence. Keys match the model id exactly, or use a '*' wildcard for a whole family (case-insensitive, e.g. 'qwen*'). Exact-id entries win over wildcard entries. Example: { "qwen*": { "temperature": 0.7, "top_p": 0.8 }, "deepseek-r1": { "temperature": 0.6 } }. Edit in settings.json.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.modelContextW.markdownDescription: Per-model context window override (total tokens), keyed by model id. Wins over what the server reports. Use when the server reports no context size — or the wrong one (e.g. llama-server router mode advertises nothing until a model is loaded). Keys match the model id exactly, or use a `*` wildcard (case-insensitive); exact-id entries win. Example: `{ "qwen2.5-coder-32b": 32768, "llama*": 123904 }`. Edit in settings.json.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.enableInlineC.markdownDescription: Experimental. Provide inline (ghost-text) code completions from your inference server, running **alongside** GitHub Copilot (not through it — VS Code does not expose bring-your-own-key models to its own inline suggestions). Requires a model/server that supports fill-in-the-middle via the `/v1/completions` `suffix` parameter (vLLM, llama.cpp, LM Studio, …). Leave disabled if you already use Copilot completions, to avoid two suggestions competing.

github.copilot.llm-gateway.inlineComplet.description: Model id to use for inline completions. Leave blank to use the first model reported by the server. Tip: point this at a small fill-in-the-middle / base model (e.g. a *-base or code FIM model) rather than a chat model for faster, cleaner completions.
Maximum tokens generated per inline completion. Lower values are faster and cheaper.
Milliseconds to wait after the last keystroke before requesting an inline completion. Higher values reduce load on the inference server while typing.
Per-request timeout (milliseconds) for inline completions. Kept short so a slow server doesn't block ghost-text suggestions.
Maximum characters of context before the cursor to send for inline completions. Lower values reduce latency but provide less context.
Maximum characters of context after the cursor to send for inline completions. Lower values reduce latency but provide less context.
